description_long: This object consists of a group of four wooden barrels resting against a large, roughly rectangular wooden lattice or fence panel. The barrels are cylindrical with visible wooden staves and reinforced by several dark metal hoops, giving them a classic coopered appearance. The barrels are arranged in a cluster, some lying on their sides and others slightly angled, suggesting storage or aging. The wooden panel behind them is weathered, with a grid-like texture and some irregular, broken edges, indicating age or outdoor exposure. The overall coloration is a mix of warm browns and yellows, with the barrels and panel both showing wood grain and signs of wear. This setup is typical of storage areas in urban or industrial environments, such as alleyways, courtyards, or the rear of commercial establishments.
description: A cluster of four wooden barrels with metal hoops rests against a large, weathered wooden lattice panel, all in warm brown tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four barrels clustered at the base of a large, textured wooden panel with visible grid pattern and broken edges.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical shape of the barrels and the thickness of the wooden panel, with two barrels most visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the full expanse of the wooden lattice panel, with only a small part of the barrels visible at the bottom.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the barrels partially obscured by the panel and the depth of the structure.
